data.title: Edgemode Delays 10-K Amid Larger 2025 Loss
data.description:
EdgeMode ( (EDGM) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Edgemode, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company says it cannot complete the filing on time without unreasonable effort or expense.

Management explains that it is still compiling the necessary financial information and facing challenges in preparing that data for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). These internal preparation issues, rather than a regulatory or auditor dispute, are the stated cause of the late filing.

Edgemode expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the automatic extension period, specifically on or before the fifteenth calendar day after the original deadline. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be submitted in mid-April, assuming no further delays.

The company is flagging a significantly larger net loss for 2025 than in 2024, driven mainly by a sharp rise in non-cash stock-based compensation and an impairment charge. Non-cash stock-based compensation alone is expected to be about $29 million in 2025, compared with zero in 2024, and the company cautions that all figures remain subject to completion of the audit and may change.

Edgemode notes that its other required SEC reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling ongoing baseline compliance despite the current delay. The notification is signed and certified on March 31, 2026, by Chief Financial Officer Simon Wajcenberg, underscoring management’s responsibility for both the late-filing notice and the forthcoming Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

More about EdgeMode

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Capstone Holding Delays 2025 Annual Financial Report
data.description:
Capstone Holding ( (CAPS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Capstone Holding Corp. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company, which remains current on all other required filings, is postponing its annual report beyond the standard March 31, 2026 deadline for smaller reporting companies.

Management cites difficulties in finishing its financial statements and related disclosures as the main cause of the delay. Its independent registered public accounting firm also needs more time to complete the audit of the 2025 financial statements to be included in the Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

Capstone Holding Corp. expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) no later than 15 calendar days after the original due date, using the extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be filed by mid-April 2026, assuming no further complications arise.

The company is signaling that 2025 results will differ meaningfully from the prior year, largely because of two acquisitions: Fraser Canyon Holdings Inc. and Continental Stone Industries Inc. These deals required complex purchase price allocations and will introduce amortization of newly acquired intangible assets into the results.

Capstone is also finalizing its annual goodwill impairment analysis, which could lead to a material non-cash charge against earnings. On top of that, the company absorbed transaction and integration costs, and dealt with capital structure changes involving Series Z preferred stock and convertible promissory notes, adding further accounting complexity.

Because audit procedures are still ongoing and several items remain unsettled, Capstone Holding Corp. says it cannot yet reasonably estimate the quantitative impact of these factors on its 2025 results. All statements about expected filing timing and potential financial effects are forward-looking and subject to change as the audit concludes.

The company emphasizes that it intends to complete its Annual Report within the permitted extension period and continue meeting its SEC reporting obligations. The notification was signed on March 31, 2026, by Chief Executive Officer Matthew E. Lipman, underscoring executive-level oversight of the filing delay and remediation steps.

The most recent analyst rating on (CAPS) stock is a Hold with a $0.57 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Capstone Holding stock, see the CAPS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAPS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAPS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (ongoing losses and renewed cash burn), with bearish technicals reinforcing downside pressure. Corporate updates provide some offset via growth/monetization actions and reduced near-term costs, but listing-compliance risk and loss-based valuation keep the overall score low.

To see Spark’s full report on CAPS stock, click here.

More about Capstone Holding

Current Market Cap: $5.9M

data.title: Datacentrex Delays Annual Report Amid Major Acquisition
data.description:
Datacentrex ( (DTCX) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Datacentrex, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will not meet the deadline for its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company’s annual report is delayed because it needs additional time to finalize and complete the audit of its 2025 annual financial statements.

The company states that the delay stems from the preparation and audit work, rather than from any disclosed internal control failure or regulator-driven review. Management indicates that the extra time is needed to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the financial information to be included in the Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

Datacentrex has committed to filing the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the 15-day extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. This means investors should expect the annual report to be submitted no later than mid-April 2026, assuming no unexpected additional delays emerge.

The company also disclosed that a significant change in its results of operations is expected compared with the prior year. This shift is driven by its December 15, 2025 acquisition of Dogehash Technologies, Inc., which is being treated as the accounting acquirer, so historical results before that date will reflect Dogehash’s operations.

Because the transaction changes which entity is considered the accounting acquirer, investors should be prepared for the 2025 figures to look different from earlier years and to reflect Dogehash’s financial profile. Any forward-looking implications of this acquisition, including growth or profitability expectations, remain subject to typical uncertainties around integration, market conditions, and execution.

The company affirms that all other required SEC periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling ongoing attention to regulatory compliance. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Parker Scott on March 31, 2026, underscoring executive-level responsibility for the delayed filing and the plan to get the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) completed within the allowed extension.

More about Datacentrex

Datacentrex, Inc., formerly Thumzup Media Corporation, is a U.S.-based company now reporting under its new name. The business recently expanded through the acquisition of Dogehash Technologies, Inc., a Nevada corporation, which is being treated as the accounting acquirer for financial reporting purposes.

Oncotelic Therapeutics, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company, formerly known as Mateon Therapeutics, Inc., is based in Agoura Hills, California, and the late filing concerns its annual report to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company explains that it is still compiling the necessary financial information required to complete the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). Management states that meeting the original deadline would have required unreasonable effort or expense, indicating internal timing and resource constraints rather than an external shock such as an auditor change or regulatory investigation.

Oncotelic Therapeutics expects to submit the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard SEC extension window. The company has indicated that it plans to file the annual report no later than the fifteenth calendar day following the prescribed due date, which is the maximum extension permitted under the relevant SEC rule for yearly reports.

The company also confirms that all other periodic filings required over the past 12 months have been made on time. Importantly for investors, Oncotelic Therapeutics does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year to be reflected in the earnings statements within the upcoming Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

That statement suggests that, while the filing is late, management does not foresee major surprises in the financials versus last year, though investors should remember that this expectation is inherently forward-looking. Actual results, once the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed, may still differ depending on final accounting judgments and audit procedures.

The notification emphasizes the company’s intent to remain in compliance with SEC reporting requirements despite the delay. data.title: Allied Gold Shareholders Overwhelmingly Back Zijin Gold Arrangement
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Allied Gold Corporation ( (TSE:AAUC) ) is now available.

Allied Gold shareholders have overwhelmingly approved a previously announced plan of arrangement with Zijin Gold International, clearing a key hurdle for the proposed transaction. More than 61% of outstanding common shares were represented at the special meeting, with over 99% of votes cast, both in total and excluding interested parties, supporting the deal.

The strong backing from shareholders signals confidence in Allied Gold’s strategic partnership with Zijin Gold, which is expected to shape the company’s future growth trajectory in Africa’s gold sector. The approval positions Allied Gold to move ahead with remaining court and regulatory steps, potentially bolstering its financial resources and competitive standing among global gold producers if the transaction is completed.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:AAUC) stock is a Hold with a C$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Allied Gold Corporation stock, see the TSE:AAUC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AAUC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AAUC is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak profitability and deteriorating free cash flow despite strong revenue growth and manageable leverage. Offsetting this, the latest earnings call was constructive with reaffirmed production guidance, strong operating cash flow, improving costs, and a solid cash position. Technically, the trend is positive, but very overbought momentum indicators increase near-term pullback risk; valuation is also pressured by losses and a negative P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on AAUC stock, click here.

More about Allied Gold Corporation

Allied Gold Corporation is a Canadian-based gold producer with a portfolio of three producing mines and development projects in Côte d’Ivoire, Mali, and Ethiopia. Led by experienced mining executives, the company is pursuing exploration, construction, and operational improvements to evolve into a mid-tier, next-generation African gold producer and ultimately a leading senior global gold company.

data.title: Dye & Durham Adopts New Rights Plan to Shield Ongoing Sales Process
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Dye & Durham ( (TSE:DND) ).

Dye & Durham has adopted a new shareholder rights plan as its existing plan nears expiry, aiming to protect the integrity of its ongoing sales process for the whole company and its Canadian Financial Services division. The move is designed to give the board more time to evaluate potential transactions in an orderly way and to ensure all shareholders are treated fairly during the review of strategic alternatives.

The rights plan, broadly aligned with similar measures at other Canadian issuers, seeks to deter creeping takeovers by restricting investors who already control 20% or more of the stock from increasing their stake outside a permitted bid. It remains subject to Toronto Stock Exchange acceptance and shareholder ratification at a special meeting expected in June 2026, after which it could stay in place for up to three years if approved.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DND) stock is a Hold with a C$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dye & Durham stock, see the TSE:DND Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DND Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DND is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—declining revenue, ongoing net losses, and especially very high leverage—despite positive (but weakening) free cash flow. Technicals are supportive due to strong recent price momentum, though overbought signals temper that benefit. Valuation is constrained by negative earnings, while the earnings call adds some offsetting positives from cost-savings and deleveraging actions, but near-term operating declines remain a key risk.

To see Spark’s full report on DND stock, click here.

More about Dye & Durham

Dye & Durham Limited is a technology company that provides practice management software for legal professionals, data insights to support corporate transactions, and payments infrastructure used by governments and financial institutions. The company operates across Canada, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, and South Africa, serving clients in highly regulated, transaction-intensive markets.

data.title: QIMC Wins Overwhelming Shareholder Support at AGM, Fortifies Governance Framework
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Quebec Silica Resources Corp. ( (TSE:QIMC) ) just unveiled an update.

Québec Innovative Materials Corp. reported that shareholders overwhelmingly backed all resolutions at its March 30 annual general and special meeting, with each motion receiving more than 99% approval. About 21.31% of outstanding shares were represented, and investors confirmed a four-member board, reappointed Baker Tilly WM LLP as auditor, and ratified both the shareholder rights plan and the share compensation plan.

Chief executive John Karagiannidis said the strong vote underscores investor confidence in the company’s governance and strategic direction as it advances its natural hydrogen portfolio in Canada and the U.S. The approval of the rights and compensation plans strengthens QIMC’s corporate framework as it pursues disciplined exploration of its district-scale projects guided by its proprietary R2G2 technical approach.

More about Quebec Silica Resources Corp.

Québec Innovative Materials Corp. is a North American exploration and development company focused on natural hydrogen and critical mineral projects. It is advancing a district-scale hydrogen exploration model across Québec, Ontario, Nova Scotia, and Minnesota using its proprietary R2G2 framework, with an emphasis on sustainable development and clean energy applications.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy
